ai_summary: Elon Musk announced Starlink's deployment on Singapore Airlines, expanding satellite internet connectivity into commercial aviation. This represents a key business milestone for Starlink—monetizing its satellite constellation through B2B partnerships with major carriers, demonstrating market traction beyond consumer broadband and advancing the company's path to profitability through enterprise use cases.
embed_html:
Starlink on Singapore Airlines! 🇸🇬 https://t.co/Y7U8fngwyz

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 4, 2026

ai_summary: Elon Musk promotes Grok, xAI's conversational AI product, with a direct link for users to try the platform. This represents a product launch/adoption push for xAI's flagship AI assistant offering, demonstrating a go-to-market strategy of direct user acquisition and engagement for a competing large language model in the AI market.
embed_html:
Try Grok https://t.co/YTb825L9Mf

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 4, 2026

text:
@teslaownersSV Any technology invention can be assessed in terms of how it improves fundamental metrics.

It is impossible to become a multiplanet civilization without reusable rockets, just as it would have been impossible to colonize America with expendable boats.

parent.text: Everyone obsesses over Starship's height and thrust. The number that actually matters: cost per kg to orbit. Falcon 9 brought it from ~$54,500 (Shuttle era) to ~$1,500. Starship targets <$100. A 500x reduction in 25 years. That's the curve enabling Mars, lunar bases and
parent.name: Tesla Owners Silicon Valley
parent.embed_html:
Everyone obsesses over Starship's height and thrust.

The number that actually matters: cost per kg to orbit.

Falcon 9 brought it from ~$54,500 (Shuttle era) to ~$1,500. Starship targets <$100.

A 500x reduction in 25 years. That's the curve enabling Mars, lunar bases and… pic.twitter.com/2lHf55hOhy

— Tesla Owners Silicon Valley (@teslaownersSV) May 3, 2026

ai_summary: The thread discusses SpaceX's fundamental achievement in reducing launch costs through reusable rocket technology, with cost per kg to orbit dropping from $54,500 (Space Shuttle era) to ~$1,500 (Falcon 9) and targeting <$100 (Starship). Elon's response articulates a critical business principle: transformative technologies must be evaluated by their impact on core unit economics rather than superficial metrics, using the historical analogy of reusable ships enabling colonization. The insight applies to SpaceX's product strategy and the economic viability of multi-planetary civilization, demonstrating how engineering breakthroughs create entirely new markets and business models.
embed_html:
Any technology invention can be assessed in terms of how it improves fundamental metrics.

It is impossible to become a multiplanet civilization without reusable rockets, just as it would have been impossible to colonize America with expendable boats.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 4, 2026
